to: consider the child’s welfare as the paramount consideration
have regard to the general principle that any delay in determining the question is likely to prejudice the welfare of the child
not make the order or any of the orders unless it considers that doing so would be better for the child than making no order at all
each have parental responsibility for the child

conditions.text: a child’s father and mother were married to, or civil partners of, each other at the time of his birth

to: have parental responsibility for the child

conditions.text: a child’s father and mother were not married to, or civil partners of, each other at the time of his birth

to: have parental responsibility for the child if he has acquired it in accordance with the provisions of this Act

conditions.text: the father shall have parental responsibility for the child if he has acquired it

to: cease to have that responsibility solely because some other person subsequently acquires parental responsibility for the child
act alone and without the other (or others) in meeting that responsibility

conditions.text: nothing in this Part shall be taken to affect the operation of any enactment which requires the consent of more than one person in a matter affecting the child

to: act in any way which would be incompatible with any order made with respect to the child under this Act
surrender or transfer any part of that responsibility to another
acquire parental responsibility for the child if he meets certain conditions
have effect unless it is made in the form prescribed by regulations made by the Lord Chancellor
have effect unless it is recorded in the prescribed manner

for: person who has acquired parental responsibility
to: cease to have that responsibility only if the court so orders
appoint an individual to be the child’s guardian

conditions.text: the child has no parent with parental responsibility for him
a parent, guardian or special guardian of the child's was named in a child arrangements order as a person with whom the child was to live and has died while the order was in force

to: appoint another individual to be the child’s guardian in the event of his death
have parental responsibility for the child concerned

for: authority or officer of the Service or a Welsh family proceedings officer
to: comply with any request for a report under this section
